entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., the crop studied for photoperiod response

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD INSENSITIVITY
entities.description: The ability of peanut genotypes to adapt across environments regardless of day length

entities.name: RESPONSE TO PHOTOPERIOD
entities.description: The difference in harvest index between short and long day treatments

entities.name: COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of parents to pass on specific traits to their offspring

entities.name: DIALLEL CROSS
entities.description: A mating design involving a six-parent cross used to study genetic control

entities.name: SHORT DAY CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental condition with shorter daylight hours used in the experiment

entities.name: LONG DAY CONDITIONS
entities.description: Environmental condition with longer daylight hours used in the experiment

entities.name: SPLIT PLOT DESIGN
entities.description: Experimental design with photoperiod as main plots and genotype as subplots

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of adjusted pod weight to adjusted biomass

entities.name: POD WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of the pods, used to calculate harvest index

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total plant mass, used to calculate harvest index

entities.name: GENERAL COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The average performance of a parent in a series of crosses, associated with response to photoperiod

entities.name: ICG2 405
entities.description: A peanut genotype identified as a good general combiner for photoperiod insensitivity

entities.name: ICGV8 6031
entities.description: A peanut genotype identified as a good general combiner for photoperiod insensitivity

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE ACTION
entities.description: A genetic interaction where the effect of alleles adds up, suggested in some crosses

entities.name: DOMINANCE
entities.description: A genetic interaction where one allele masks another, suggested in some crosses
